hypothesis: If a language model is fine-tuned on BCC/B2 composition data via supervised learning and then optimized with Direct Preference Optimization using a Thermo-Calc–based hierarchical reward that favors BCC/B2 phase coexistence, early BCC formation, room-temperature B2 presence, and low non-BCC/B2 content (with lattice-mismatch as a quality metric), then the model will generate alloy candidates with higher thermodynamic feasibility and lower non-BCC/B2 content than baseline methods, because the physics-informed reward directly encodes the target phase constraints guiding generation.
